meta.description: Production-ready n8n blueprint for Codex: chat agent, document training, document Q&A, people directory, web research, image fetch, code assistant, OpenAI primary model, Gemini backup model.

nodes.name: 01 Chat Trigger - Frontend or n8n Chat

parameters.toolDescription: Search approved ITsNomatata internal documents, policies, uploaded files, project summaries, and GraphRAG knowledge. Check role/department scope before using the content.

Retrieve long-term private memory for the current user and, when present, the current project. Use this before answering questions that depend on prior preferences, decisions, project context, recurring work, or what the user previously told Codex. Never retrieve another user's memory.
parameters.url: ={{ (() => { const base = $vars.SUPABASE_URL || 'https://zirftywinscopzuuwdlg.supabase.co'; const org = $json.context.organizationId; const user = $json.context.userId; const project = $json.metadata?.projectId; const scope = project ? `&or=(project_id.is.null,project_id.eq.${encodeURIComponent(project)})` : '&project_id=is.null'; return `${base}/rest/v1/ai_memory_items?select=*&organization_id=eq.${org}&user_id=eq.${user}${scope}&order=pinned.desc,importance.desc,updated_at.desc&limit=16`; })() }}

parameters.toolDescription: Save useful long-term memory for the current user only. Save preferences, stable facts, project decisions, recurring instructions, and concise summaries that will help future chats. Do not save secrets, credentials, medical/legal sensitive data, or other people's private history. Use project scope when metadata.projectId exists.
Look up approved team members, departments, roles, responsibilities, managers, and active status from Supabase. Never invent people.

Research external companies using Tavily Search API. Prefer official websites and reliable sources. Return title, description, services, contact details, social links, images, and source URLs. Do not use random unsourced content.
Fetch company-related images from official websites: og:image, logo, favicon, hero images, gallery images, product images. Return source URLs and avoid random unsourced images.

Analyze an uploaded image using OpenAI vision. Use this when the user sends a picture and asks what it is, what car/model/object appears, to read text from an image, or to extract visible details. Input must include an image URL from the current attachments and a focused question. Return observations, likely identification, visible text, confidence, and limits.

parameters.name: input
parameters.value: ={{ [{ role: 'user', content: [{ type: 'input_text', text: $fromAI('image_question', 'Analyze this image. Identify visible objects, text, brand/model clues, and important details. Be honest about uncertainty.', 'string') }, { type: 'input_image', image_url: $fromAI('image_url', ($json.attachments || []).find(a => a?.url)?.url || '', 'string') }] }] }}

parameters.toolDescription: Generate a downloadable PDF from an AI answer, document summary, report, or HTML. Input should include title, html or markdown/content, file_name, organization_id, user_id, and optional storage target. Return attachment object with type pdf, url, and download_url.

Create downloadable txt, md, json, csv, or html files from Codex output. Save to Supabase Storage or Google Drive through the file service. Return attachment object with type, name, url, and download_url.

Generate an image with OpenAI. Use this only when the user asks to create a new image. Improve the prompt for clean business use. The OpenAI response returns base64 image data in data[0].b64_json; convert it into an attachment with url and download_url set to data:image/png;base64,{b64_json}. Never claim success without returning that visible image attachment.

options.systemMessage: Paste the full prompt from n8n/itsnomatata-ai-workspace-brain.system-prompt.md here. OpenAI is the primary model. Use tools only when needed. Respect role, department, organization, and project memory scope. If verified internal knowledge is missing, say so clearly.

nodes.name: Document Training Trigger - Upload / Manual / Drive

parameters.jsCode:
const source = $('05 Permission + Tool Router').first().json;
return [{ json: { success: true, type: 'text', message: 'Please upload or select the image you want me to edit.', output: 'Please upload or select the image you want me to edit.', conversationId: source.conversationId || null, attachments: [], sources: [] } }];

const source = $('05 Permission + Tool Router').first().json;
return [{ json: { success: true, type: 'ocr_extraction', message: 'Please upload the document or image you want me to read.', output: 'Please upload the document or image you want me to read.', conversationId: source.conversationId || null, attachments: [], sources: [] } }];
